reviews.title: AI is dangerous—and ChatGPT is funding against AI regulations
reviews.content: They are using their profits to push against necessary AI regulations. Don’t let them win. Delete the app.

reviews.title: Not worth it
reviews.content: Wasting our fresh drinking water, colluding with a fascist regime, and eroding our collective critical thinking skills in advancement of a product that outputs wrong information.

reviews.title: Help Save Water
reviews.content: Data centers have expanded rapidly over the past few years. They take up lots of our resources. When water is over used, they still pay less than when we do it. Stop the data center spread before it’s too late.

reviews.title: I need help signing in
reviews.content: I downloaded the app and I’m trying to sign in. I can’t because I have to have a code that is sent to my email. By the time the code arrives the page to enter the code is gone.

reviews.title: Not impressed
reviews.content: I used it once today and it told me I reached my limit and needed to upgrade. It has done that several times over the last 2 weeks. I use it once then it tells me I should upgrade I reached my limit for the day. Seems more like a scam to force people to pay for the service. So I deleted it.

reviews.content: Промты на обработку фото перестали корректно работать после обновления, ужасно все стало

reviews.title: Is actively siding with scammers
reviews.content: I tried to make it store memories against these scam games and apps it was saying that everything I said was false even though it was 100% true. Please fix this

reviews.title: Harmful to the environment
reviews.content: ChatGPT uses more than 500,000 kilowatts of energy every day, which is enough energy to power 180,000 homes. ChatGPT relies on data centers which emit around 8.4 tons of Carbon Dioxide every year, which is VERY harmful to the environment since Carbon Dioxide is a greenhouse gas. ChatGPT also uses around 85,000 gallons of water per day since the data centers it uses need water to be cooled, and the amount of water used by asking ChatGPT to write a 100 word essay is more than a standard 16 oz water bottle. ChatGPT and other generative AI are VERY harmful to the environment and it's super important people stop using it.

reviews.title: the dumbest ai
reviews.content: chatgpt lacks critical thinking and accuracy. other ais can do what chatgpt can’t, that’s it

reviews.title: BOYCOTT CHATGPT
reviews.content: it’s not worth supporting MAGA and ICE. by supporting chatgpt you are supporting destroying and hurting vulnerable families.

reviews.title: THE WORST OF GEN AI
reviews.content: ChatGPT is everything that is wrong with AI. A worthless model that is more frustrating than helpful, a founder is is actively trying to destroy humanity, and zero moral compass.

reviews.title: Usage limits
reviews.content: I absolutely love how Claude AI functions but the reason for my low rating is the usage limits! It’s like back to 2008 Verizon data limits. I can’t much without hitting my limit even on the pro plan then I have to wait 5 hours to talk again? Even then there are conversation size limits plus the chats within projects don’t talk to each other so you have to completely start over. If all this was resolved this would be far superior to ChatGPT.

reviews.title: Claudossis
reviews.content:
There are limits on every level of "subscription" and on every type of use, be it simple chatting or heavy project development. 
People have written numerous articles  about how they have basically changed their work habits or lives in order to adapt to Claude's 'brutal' limits.  Evidently the Forbin Project is doing well in the form of the AI God Claude.

reviews.title: Needs a basic account switcher
reviews.content: The Anthropic team ships so much value but misses some important basic features for productivity. Add the ability to toggle between accounts. I can’t do my personal stuff on my work account. Half of us have a side hustle. Stop holding me back.

reviews.title: Fatal Bugs in UI
reviews.content:
Well, they fixed the problem from my original 1-star review, but now there are bugs that are even worse. As Claude admitted today (30 April 2026), there is a known bug in the app that can sometimes prevent selecting and copying text from an answer more than one paragraph at a time. Try that with the 8000-word text you spent two days creating, or a 1000-line computer program. The suggested work-around was to log into your account on the Claude website.

Unfortunately, that is impossible if you used your Apple ID to create your account on the app — you can log in with an email or Google, but Apple users are told to download the app… which is unusable. When I pointed this out, Claude kindly suggested that I file a bug report via their support website. Except that you cannot file a report without logging in… which app users who used their Apple ID cannot do.

The app was updated today. The selection problem persists.

Former review: I found it almost impossible to use this app because I ke

This is how it goes. “ make me this with that” on chrome extension. Doesnt do what i said and by the time its almost there it says i hit my limit.



Not enough time. Dont get me wrong . This is a very advanced new ai. I was just wishing more than a 5 hour limit. Does good things but craps out after some semi big mini projects.

I was using cowork on my pc and phone to assist with new construction take off, reading plans catching key notes I missed and items that get lost in multi level/multi family new builds highly complex projects that require multiple estimators to review, Claude made me a one man army, a real force multiplier.

Now it won’t follow the prompts I give it, hallucinating items and measurements, actually making things up that don’t exist in the plans and straight up just half baked results. Leaving me a state of frustration. 

I was paying the pro max subscription $249.00 a month and honestly I’d rather just hire another human to assist me. Un real the disappointment I have, no idea what happened to this product but it is not what it used to be at all.

reviews.content: This is like my fourth review I think? I’ve been liking Gemini AI model lately and I think it does particularly well with nutrition and health related topics. Occasionally you’ll also see Gemini hysterically burst out random thinking out loud outputs, but that’s one of the lesser hilarities from the app. Worst yet, I don’t even know how to properly submit my crash reports and I’m not even sure if they’re tracked. If you can’t trust Gemini to give a correct answer to a 1st grade question, then you can’t trust it at all.

reviews.title: Don’t Be Evil
reviews.content:
Google tracks and sells your activity. 

They started the company with the motto “don’t be evil”. Then a few years went by and they quietly dropped the motto while switching to more aggressive marketing strategies. Today they are one of the most destructive forces on Earth. Their algorithms determine what people see when we search and therefore shapes our understanding of the world. They use this to their advantage by feeding us the version of the world that they believe in. How much of the strife in our modern lives is due to these nefarious corporate tactics to influence the world according to their interests? 

Don’t be evil.
